text.KJV: Howbeit wee would not be troublesome vnto you, nor to others of our confederates & friends in these warres:
text.NABRE: We did not wish to be troublesome to you and to the rest of our allies and friends in these wars.
text.NRSV: We were unwilling to annoy you and our other allies and friends with these wars,

text.DRB: For we have had aid from heaven, and we are delivered, and our enemies are humbled.
text.KJV: For wee haue helpe from heauen that succoureth vs, so as we are deliuered from our enemies, and our enemies are brought vnder foote.
text.NABRE: For we have the help of Heaven for our support, and we have been saved from our enemies, and our enemies have been humbled.
text.NRSV: for we have the help that comes from Heaven for our aid, and so we were delivered from our enemies, and our enemies were humbled.

text.DRB: We have chosen therefore Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the son of Jason, and have sent to the Romans to renew with them the old amity and society.
text.KJV: For this cause we chose Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the sonne of Iason, and sent them vnto the Romanes, to renew the amitie that we had with them, and the former league.
text.NABRE: So we have chosen Numenius, son of Antiochus, and Antipater, son of Jason, and we have sent them to the Romans to renew with them the friendship and alliance of earlier times.
text.NRSV: We therefore have chosen Numenius son of Antiochus and Antipater son of Jason, and have sent them to Rome to renew our former friendship and alliance with them.

text.DRB: We therefore have commanded them that they come unto you also, and salute you, and deliver you our epistles, concerning the renewing of our fraternity.
text.KJV: We commanded them also to goe vnto you, and to salute you, and to deliuer you our letters, concerning the renewing of our brotherhood.
text.NABRE: We have also ordered them to come to you and greet you, and to deliver to you our letter concerning the renewal of our brotherhood.
text.NRSV: We have commanded them to go also to you and greet you and deliver to you this letter from us concerning the renewal of our family ties.

text.DRB: And now you shall do well in answering us to these things.
text.KJV: Wherefore now ye shall doe well to giue vs an answere thereto.
text.NABRE: Therefore kindly send us an answer on this matter.”
text.NRSV: And now please send us a reply to this.’

text.DRB: And this is the rescript of the epistles, which he had sent to Onias:
text.KJV: And this is the copy of the letters which Omiares sent:
text.NABRE: This is a copy of the letter that they sent to Onias:
text.NRSV: This is a copy of the letter that they sent to Onias:

text.DRB: Arius the king of the Spartians to Onias the grand Priest greeting.
text.KJV: Areus king of the Lacedemonians, to Onias the hie Priest, greeting.
text.NABRE: “Arius, king of the Spartans, sends greetings to Onias the high priest.
text.NRSV: ‘King Arius of the Spartans, to the high priest Onias, greetings.

text.DRB: It is found in scripture of the Spartians, and the Jews, that they are brethren, & that they are of the stock of Abraham.
text.KJV: It is found in writing, that the Lacedemonians and Iewes are brethren, and that they are of the stocke of Abraham:
text.NABRE: A document has been found stating that the Spartans and the Jews are brothers and that they are of the family of Abraham.
text.NRSV: It has been found in writing concerning the Spartans and the Jews that they are brothers and are of the family of Abraham.

text.DRB: And now since we understood these things, you do well in writing to us of your peace.
text.KJV: Now therefore, since this is come to our knowledge, you shall doe well to write vnto vs of your prosperitie.
text.NABRE: Now that we have learned this, kindly write to us about your welfare.
text.NRSV: And now that we have learned this, please write us concerning your welfare;

text.DRB: Yea and we have written again to you, Our cattle, and our possessions, are yours: and yours, ours. We therefore have commanded that these things be told you.
text.KJV: We doe write backe againe to you, that your cattell and goods are ours, and ours are yours. We doe command therefore to make report vnto you on this wise.
text.NABRE: We, for our part, declare to you that your animals and your possessions are ours, and ours are yours. We have, therefore, given orders that you should be told of this.”
text.NRSV: we on our part write to you that your livestock and your property belong to us, and ours belong to you. We therefore command that our envoys report to you accordingly.’

text.DRB: And Jonathas heard that the princes of Demetrius were come again with a great army more than before to fight against him.
text.KJV: Now when Ionathan heard that Demetrius princes were come to fight against him with a greater hoste then afore,
text.NABRE: More Campaigns of Jonathan and Simon. Then Jonathan heard that the officers of Demetrius had returned to attack him with a stronger army than before.
text.NRSV: Further Campaigns of Jonathan and Simon Now Jonathan heard that the commanders of Demetrius had returned, with a larger force than before, to wage war against him.

text.DRB: And he went out from Jerusalem, & met them in the country of Amathis: for he gave them not space to enter into his country.
text.KJV: Hee remooued from Ierusalem, and met them in the land of Amathis: for he gaue them no respite to enter his countrey.
text.NABRE: So he set out from Jerusalem and met them in the territory of Hamath, giving them no opportunity to enter his province.
text.NRSV: So he marched away from Jerusalem and met them in the region of Hamath, for he gave them no opportunity to invade his own country.

text.DRB: And he sent spies into their camp, and they returning reported that they appointed to come upon them in the night.
text.KJV: He sent spies also vnto their tents, who came againe, and tolde him, that they were appointed to come vpon them in the night season.
text.NABRE: The spies he had sent into their camp came back and reported to him that the enemy were preparing to attack them that night.
text.NRSV: He sent spies to their camp, and they returned and reported to him that the enemy were being drawn up in formation to attack the Jews by night.

text.DRB: And when the sun was set, Jonathas commanded his men to watch, and to be in armour ready to fight all night, and he set watchmen round about the camp.
text.KJV: Wherefore so soone as the Sunne was downe, Ionathan commaunded his men to watch, and to be in armes, that all the night long they might bee ready to fight: Also he sent foorth sentinels round about the hoste.
text.NABRE: Therefore, when the sun set, Jonathan ordered his men to keep watch, with their weapons at the ready for battle, throughout the night; and he set outposts around the camp.
text.NRSV: So when the sun had set, Jonathan commanded his troops to be alert and to keep their arms at hand so as to be ready all night for battle, and he stationed outposts around the camp.

text.DRB: And the adversaries heard that Jonathas with his company was prepared to battle: & they feared & were afraid in their heart: and they kindled fires in their camp.
text.KJV: But when the aduersaries heard that Ionathan and his men were ready for battell, they feared, and trembled in their hearts, and they kindled fires in their campe.
text.NABRE: When the enemy heard that Jonathan and his men were ready for battle, their hearts sank with fear and dread. They lighted fires in their camp and then withdrew.
text.NRSV: When the enemy heard that Jonathan and his troops were prepared for battle, they were afraid and were terrified at heart; so they kindled fires in their camp and withdrew.

text.DRB: But Jonathas, & they that were with him, knew it not till the morning, and they saw light burning,
text.KJV: Howbeit Ionathan and his company knew it not till the morning: for they saw the lights burning.
text.NABRE: But because Jonathan and his men were watching the campfires burning, they did not know until the morning what had happened.
text.NRSV: But Jonathan and his troops did not know it until morning, for they saw the fires burning.

text.DRB: and Jonathas followed them, & overtook them not: for they had passed the river Eleutherus.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan pursued after them, but ouertooke them not: for they were gone ouer the riuer Eleutherus.
text.NABRE: Then Jonathan pursued them, but he could not overtake them, for they had crossed the river Eleutherus.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan pursued them, but he did not overtake them, for they had crossed the Eleutherus river.

text.DRB: And Jonathas turned aside to the Arabians, that were called Zabadeans, & he struck them, & took the spoils of them.
text.KJV: Wherefore Ionathan turned to the Arabians, who were called Zabadeans, and smote them, and tooke their spoiles.
text.NABRE: So Jonathan turned aside against the Arabians who are called Zabadeans, and he struck them down and plundered them.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan turned aside against the Arabs who are called Zabadeans, and he crushed them and plundered them.

text.DRB: And he joined, & came to Damascus, & walked through all that country.
text.KJV: And remouing thence, he came to Damascus, and so passed through all the countrey.
text.NABRE: Then he broke camp, marched on toward Damascus and traveled through the whole region.
text.NRSV: Then he broke camp and went to Damascus, and marched through all that region.

text.DRB: But Simon went forth, and came even to Ascalon, and to the next holds, and he declined into Joppe, and took it.
text.KJV: Simon also went foorth, and passed through the countrey vnto Ascalon, and the holds there adioyning, from whence he turned aside to Ioppe, and wanne it.
text.NABRE: Simon also set out and traveled as far as Askalon and its neighboring strongholds. He then turned to Joppa and took it by surprise,
text.NRSV: Simon also went out and marched through the country as far as Askalon and the neighbouring strongholds. He turned aside to Joppa and took it by surprise,

text.DRB: (For he heard that they would deliver the hold to Demetrius' part) and he put a garrison there to keep it.
text.KJV: For he had heard that they would deliuer the hold vnto them that tooke Demetrius part, wherefore he set a garison there to keepe it.
text.NABRE: for he heard that its people intended to hand over the stronghold to the supporters of Demetrius. He left a garrison there to guard it.
text.NRSV: for he had heard that they were ready to hand over the stronghold to those whom Demetrius had sent. And he stationed a garrison there to guard it.

text.DRB: And Jonathas returned, and called together the ancients of the people, & he consulted with them to build fortresses in Jurie,
text.KJV: After this came Ionathan home againe, and calling the Elders of the people together, hee consulted with them about building strong holdes in Iudea,
text.NABRE: When Jonathan returned, he assembled the elders of the people, and with them he made plans for building strongholds in Judea,
text.NRSV: When Jonathan returned he convened the elders of the people and planned with them to build strongholds in Judea,

text.DRB: & to build the walls in Jerusalem, and to rear a mount of a great height between the castle and the city, to separate it from the city, that it might be alone, and they might neither buy nor sell:
text.KJV: And making the walles of Ierusalem higher, and raising a great mount betweene the towre and the city, for to separate it from the city, that so it might be alone, that men might neither sell nor buy in it.
text.NABRE: for making the walls of Jerusalem still higher, and for erecting a high barrier between the citadel and the city, to separate it from the city and isolate it, so that its garrison could neither buy nor sell.
text.NRSV: to build the walls of Jerusalem still higher, and to erect a high barrier between the citadel and the city to separate it from the city, in order to isolate it so that its garrison could neither buy nor sell.

text.DRB: And they came together to build the city: and the wall fell that was upon the torrent toward the rising of the sun, and he repaired it, which is called Caphetetha:
text.KJV: Upon this they came together, to build vp the citie forasmuch as the wall toward the brooke on the East side was fallen down, & they repaired that which was called Caphenatha
text.NABRE: The people therefore gathered together to build up the city, for part of the wall of the eastern valley had collapsed. And Jonathan repaired the quarter called Chaphenatha.
text.NRSV: So they gathered together to rebuild the city; part of the wall on the valley to the east had fallen, and he repaired the section called Chaphenatha.

text.DRB: and Simon built Adiada in Sephela, and fortified it, and hanged on gates & locks.
text.KJV: Simon also set vp Adida, in Sephela, and made it strong with gates and barres.
text.NABRE: Simon likewise built up Adida in the Shephelah, and fortified it by installing gates and bars.
text.NRSV: Simon also built Adida in the Shephelah; he fortified it and installed gates with bolts.

text.DRB: And when Tryphon had purposed to reign over Asia, and to take the crown, and to extend his hand against Antiochus the king:
text.KJV: Now Tryphon went about to get the kingdome of Asia, and to kill Antiochus the king, that hee might set the crowne vpon his owne head.
text.NABRE: Capture of Jonathan. Then Trypho sought to become king of Asia, assume the diadem, and do violence to King Antiochus.
text.NRSV: Trypho Captures Jonathan Then Trypho attempted to become king in Asia and put on the crown, and to raise his hand against King Antiochus.

text.DRB: fearing lest perhaps Jonathas would not permit him, but would fight against him, he sought to take him and to kill him. And rising up he went into Bethsan.
text.KJV: Howbeit, he was afraid that Ionathan would not suffer him, and that he would fight against him, wherefore he sought a way, howe to take Ionathan, that he might kill him. So he remoued, and came to Bethsan.
text.NABRE: But he was afraid that Jonathan would not permit him, but would fight against him. Looking for a way to seize and kill him, he set out and came to Beth-shan.
text.NRSV: He feared that Jonathan might not permit him to do so, but might make war on him, so he kept seeking to seize and kill him, and he marched out and came to Beth-shan.

text.DRB: And Jonathas went forth to meet him with forty thousand chosen men into battle, and came to Bethsan.
text.KJV: Then Ionathan went out to meet him with fourtie thousand men, chosen for the battell, and came to Bethsan.
text.NABRE: Jonathan marched out to meet him with forty thousand picked fighting men and came to Beth-shan.
text.NRSV: Jonathan went out to meet him with forty thousand picked warriors, and he came to Beth-shan.

text.DRB: And Tryphon saw that Jonathas came with a great army, to extend his hands upon him: and he feared:
text.KJV: Now when Tryphon saw that Ionathan came with so great a force, hee durst not stretch his hande against him,
text.NABRE: But when Trypho saw that Jonathan had arrived with a large army he was afraid to do him violence.
text.NRSV: When Trypho saw that he had come with a large army, he was afraid to raise his hand against him.

text.DRB: and received him with honour, & commanded him to all his friends, and gave him gifts: and he commanded his hosts to obey him, as himself.
text.KJV: But receiued him honourably, and cōmended him vnto all his friends, and gaue him gifts, and commaunded his men of warre to be as obedient vnto him, as to himselfe.
text.NABRE: Instead, he received him with honor, introduced him to all his friends, and gave him presents. He also ordered his friends and soldiers to obey him as they would himself.
text.NRSV: So he received him with honour and commended him to all his Friends, and he gave him gifts and commanded his Friends and his troops to obey him as they would himself.

text.DRB: And he said to Jonathas: Why hast thou troubled all the people, whereas we have no war?
text.KJV: Unto Ionathan also hee said, Why hast thou put all this people to so great trouble, seeing there is no warre betwixt vs?
text.NABRE: Then he said to Jonathan: “Why have you put all these people to so much trouble when we are not at war?
text.NRSV: Then he said to Jonathan, ‘Why have you put all these people to so much trouble when we are not at war?

text.DRB: And now send them back to their houses, but choose thee a few men, that may be with thee, and come with me to Ptolomais, and I will deliver it to thee, and the other holds, and the army, and all the overseers of the affairs, and returning I will depart: for therefore I came.
text.KJV: Therefore send them now home againe, and chuse a few men to waite on thee, and come thou with me to Ptolemais, for I will giue it thee and the rest of the strong holds and forces, and all that haue any charge: as for me, I will returne and depart: for this is the cause of my comming.
text.NABRE: Now pick out a few men to stay with you, send the rest to their homes, and then come with me to Ptolemais. I will hand it over to you together with other strongholds and the remaining troops, as well as all the officials; then I will turn back and go home. That is why I came here.”
text.NRSV: Dismiss them now to their homes and choose for yourself a few men to stay with you, and come with me to Ptolemais. I will hand it over to you as well as the other strongholds and the remaining troops and all the officials, and will turn around and go home. For that is why I am here.’

text.DRB: And Jonathas believed him, and did as he said: and dismissed his army, and they departed into the land of Juda:
text.KJV: So Ionathan beleeuing him, did as he bade him, and sent away his host, who went into the land of Iudea.
text.NABRE: Jonathan trusted him and did as he said. He dismissed his troops, and they returned to the land of Judah.
text.NRSV: Jonathan trusted him and did as he said; he sent away the troops, and they returned to the land of Judah.

text.DRB: but he retained with him three thousand men: of whom he sent back two thousand into Galilee, and a thousand came with him.
text.KJV: And with himselfe hee retained but three thousand men, of whome he sent two thousand into Galile, and one thousand went with him.
text.NABRE: But he kept with him three thousand men, of whom he left two thousand in Galilee while one thousand accompanied him.
text.NRSV: He kept with himself three thousand men, two thousand of whom he left in Galilee, while one thousand accompanied him.

text.KJV: Now assoone as Ionathan entred into Ptolemais, they of Ptolemais shut the gates, and tooke him, and all them that came with him, they shewe with the sword.
text.NABRE: Then as soon as Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him; all who had entered with him, they killed with the sword.
text.NRSV: But when Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him, and they killed with the sword all who had entered with him.
text.DRB: But as Jonathas entered into Ptolemais, they of Ptolemais shut the gates of the city, and they took him, and all that had entered with him they slew with the sword.
And Tryphon sent an army and horsemen into Galilee, and into the great plain to destroy all Jonathas' fellows.
text.KJV: Then sent Tryphon an hoste of footmen, and horsemen into Galile, and into the great plaine, to destroy all Ionathans company.
text.NABRE: Then Trypho sent soldiers and cavalry to Galilee and the Great Plain to destroy all Jonathan’s men.
text.NRSV: Then Trypho sent troops and cavalry into Galilee and the Great Plain to destroy all Jonathan’s soldiers.

text.DRB: But they when they understood that Jonathas was taken, & was perished, and all that were with him, they encouraged themselves, and went forth ready to battle.
text.KJV: But when they knew that Ionathan and they that were with him were taken and slaine, they encouraged one another, and went close together, prepared to fight.
text.NABRE: These, upon learning that Jonathan had been captured and killed along with his companions, encouraged one another and went out in close formation, ready to fight.
text.NRSV: But they realized that Jonathan had been seized and had perished along with his men, and they encouraged one another and kept marching in close formation, ready for battle.

text.DRB: And these that had pursued, seeing that the matter stood upon their life, they returned.
text.KJV: They therfore that followed vpon them, perceiuing þ they were ready to fight for their liues, turned back againe.
text.NABRE: As their pursuers saw that they were ready to fight for their lives, they turned back.
text.NRSV: When their pursuers saw that they would fight for their lives, they turned back.

text.DRB: But they came all with peace into the land of Juda. And they lamented Jonathas, & them that were with him exceedingly: and Israel mourned with great mourning.
text.KJV: Whereupon they all came into the land of Iudea peaceably, and there they bewailed Ionathan & them that were with him, & they were sore afraid, wherfore all Israel made great lamentation.
text.NABRE: Thus all Jonathan’s men came safely into the land of Judah. They mourned Jonathan and those who were with him. They were in great fear, and all Israel fell into deep mourning.
text.NRSV: So they all reached the land of Judah safely, and they mourned for Jonathan and his companions and were in great fear; and all Israel mourned deeply.

text.DRB: And all the heathen that were round about them, sought to destroy them, for they said:
text.KJV: Then all the heathen that were round about them, sought to destroy them. For, said they, they haue no captaine, nor any to helpe them. Now therfore let vs make war vpon them, & take away their memorial frō amongst men.
text.NABRE: All the nations round about sought to crush them. They said, “Now that they have no leader or helper, let us make war on them and wipe out their memory from the earth.”
text.NRSV: All the nations around them tried to destroy them, for they said, ‘They have no leader or helper. Now therefore let us make war on them and blot out the memory of them from humankind.’

text.DRB: They have no prince, and helper: now therefore let us expugn them, and take away from men the memory of them.

text.KJV: Now when Simon heard that Tryphon had gathered together a great hoste to inuade the land of Iudea, and destroy it,
text.NABRE: IV. Leadership of Simon Chapter 13 Simon as Leader. When Simon heard that Trypho was gathering a large army to invade and ravage the land of Judah,
text.NRSV: Simon Takes Command 13
text.DRB: And Simon heard that Tryphon gathered a very great army, to come into the land of Juda, and to destroy it.
Seeing that the people was in dread, and in fear, he went up to Jerusalem, and assembled the people:
text.KJV: And saw that the people was in great trembling and feare, he went vp to Ierusalem, and gathered the people together,
text.NABRE: and saw that the people were trembling with terror, he went up to Jerusalem. There he assembled the people
text.NRSV: and he saw that the people were trembling with fear. So he went up to Jerusalem, and gathering the people together

text.DRB: and exhorting he said: You know what great battles I and my brethren, and the house of my father have made for the laws, and the holies, and the distresses that we have seen:
text.KJV: And gaue them exhortation, saying: Yee your selues know, what great things I and my brethren, and my fathers house haue done for the lawes, and the Sanctuarie, the battels also, and troubles which we haue seene,
text.NABRE: and exhorted them in these words: “You know what I, my brothers, and my father’s house have done for the laws and the sanctuary; what battles and hardships we have seen.
text.NRSV: he encouraged them, saying to them, ‘You yourselves know what great things my brothers and I and the house of my father have done for the laws and the sanctuary; you know also the wars and the difficulties that my brothers and I have seen.

text.DRB: by reason of these are all my brethren perished for Israel, and I only am left.
text.KJV: By reason whereof all my brethren are slaine for Israels sake, and I am left alone.
text.NABRE: For the sake of this, for the sake of Israel, all my brothers have perished, and I alone am left.
text.NRSV: By reason of this all my brothers have perished for the sake of Israel, and I alone am left.

text.DRB: And now let it not fall to me to spare my life in all time of tribulation: for I am not better than my brethren.
text.KJV: Now therefore be it farre from me, that I should spare mine owne life in any time of trouble: for I am no better then my brethren.
text.NABRE: Far be it from me, then, to save my own life in any time of distress, for I am not better than my brothers.
text.NRSV: And now, far be it from me to spare my life in any time of distress, for I am not better than my brothers.

text.DRB: I will avenge therefore my nation and the holies, our children also, and wives: because all the heathen are gathered together to destroy us of enmity.
text.KJV: Doubtlesse I will auenge my nation and the Sanctuarie, & our wiues, and our children: for all the heathen are gathered to destroy vs, of very malice.
text.NABRE: But I will avenge my nation and the sanctuary, as well as your wives and children, for out of hatred all the Gentiles have united to crush us.”
text.NRSV: But I will avenge my nation and the sanctuary and your wives and children, for all the nations have gathered together out of hatred to destroy us.’

text.DRB: And the spirit of the people was kindled as soon as they heard these words:
text.KJV: Now as soone as the people heard these words, their spirit reuiued.
text.NABRE: As the people heard these words, their spirit was rekindled.
text.NRSV: The spirit of the people was rekindled when they heard these words,

text.KJV: And they answered with a loud voice, saying, Thou shalt bee our leader in stead of Iudas and Ionathan thy brother.
text.NABRE: They shouted in reply: “You are our leader in place of your brothers Judas and Jonathan.
text.NRSV: and they answered in a loud voice, ‘You are our leader in place of Judas and your brother Jonathan.
text.DRB: and they answered with a loud voice, saying: Thou art our captain in the place of Judas, and Jonathas thy brother:
fight thou our battle, and we will do all things whatsoever thou shalt say to us.
text.KJV: Fight thou our battels, & what soeuer thou commandest vs, that will we doe.
text.NABRE: Fight our battles, and we will do everything that you tell us.”
text.NRSV: Fight our battles, and all that you say to us we will do.’

text.DRB: And gathering together all the men of war, he hastened to finish all the walls of Jerusalem, and fortified it round about.
text.KJV: So then he gathered together all the men of warre, and made hast to finish the walles of Ierusalem, and he fortified it round about.
text.NABRE: So Simon mustered all the men able to fight, and hastening to complete the walls of Jerusalem, fortified it on every side.
text.NRSV: So he assembled all the warriors and hurried to complete the walls of Jerusalem, and he fortified it on every side.

text.DRB: And he sent Jonathas the son of Absalom, and with him a new army into Joppe, and they that were in the castle being cast out, he remained there.
text.KJV: Also he sent Ionathan, the sonne of Absolom, & with him a great power to Ioppe, who casting out them that were therein, remained there in it.
text.NABRE: He sent Jonathan, son of Absalom, to Joppa with a strong force; Jonathan drove out the occupants and remained there.
text.NRSV: He sent Jonathan son of Absalom to Joppa, and with him a considerable army; he drove out its occupants and remained there.

text.DRB: And Tryphon removed from Ptolemais with a great army, to come into the land of Juda, and Jonathas with him in ward.
text.KJV: So Tryphon remoued from Ptolemais, with a great power to inuade the land of Iudea, and Ionathan was with him in warde.
text.NABRE: Trypho’s Deceit. Then Trypho moved from Ptolemais with a large army to invade the land of Judah, bringing Jonathan with him as a prisoner.
text.NRSV: Deceit and Treachery of Trypho Then Trypho left Ptolemais with a large army to invade the land of Judah, and Jonathan was with him under guard.

text.DRB: But Simon pitched in Addus against the face of the plain.
text.KJV: But Simon pitched his tents at Adida, ouer against the plaine.
text.NABRE: Simon encamped at Adida, facing the plain.
text.NRSV: Simon encamped in Adida, facing the plain.

text.DRB: And as Tryphon understood that Simon was risen in the place of his brother Jonathas: and that he would join battle with him, he sent legates to him,
text.KJV: Now when Tryphon knew that Simon, was risen vp in stead of his brother Ionathan, and meant to ioyne battell with him, he sent messengers vnto him, saying,
text.NABRE: When Trypho learned that Simon had succeeded his brother Jonathan, and that he intended to fight him, he sent ambassadors to him with this message:
text.NRSV: Trypho learned that Simon had risen up in place of his brother Jonathan, and that he was about to join battle with him, so he sent envoys to him and said,

text.DRB: saying: For the silver, that thy brother Jonathas owed in the king's account, for the affairs which he had, we have stayed him.
text.KJV: Whereas we haue Ionathan thy brother in hold, it is for money that he is owing vnto the kings treasure, concerning the businesse that was committed vnto him.
text.NABRE: “It was on account of the money your brother Jonathan owed the royal treasury in connection with the offices that he held, that we have detained him.
text.NRSV: ‘It is for the money that your brother Jonathan owed the royal treasury, in connection with the offices he held, that we are detaining him.

text.DRB: And now send an hundred talents of silver, and his two sons for hostages, that being dismissed he fly not from us, and we will release him.
text.KJV: Wherefore, now send an hundred talents of siluer, and two of his sonnes for hostages, that when he is at liberty he may not reuolt from vs, and we will let him goe.
text.NABRE: Now send a hundred talents of silver, and two of his sons as hostages to guarantee that when he is set free he will not revolt against us, and we will release him.”
text.NRSV: Send now one hundred talents of silver and two of his sons as hostages, so that when released he will not revolt against us, and we will release him.’

text.DRB: And Simon knew that with guile he spake with him, nevertheless he commanded the silver, and the children to be given: lest he should get great enmity with the people of Israel, saying:
text.KJV: Heereupon Simon, albeit he perceiued that they spake deceiptfully vnto him, yet sent he the money, and the children, lest peraduenture he should procure to himselfe great hatred of the people:
text.NABRE: Simon knew that they were speaking deceitfully to him. Nevertheless, for fear of provoking much hostility among the people, he sent for the money and the boys,
text.NRSV: Simon knew that they were speaking deceitfully to him, but he sent to get the money and the sons, so that he would not arouse great hostility among the people, who might say,

text.DRB: Because he sent not the silver, and the children, therefore is he perished.
text.KJV: Who might haue said, Because I sent him not the money, and the children, therefore is dead.
text.NABRE: lest the people say “Jonathan perished because I would not send Trypho the money and the boys.”
text.NRSV: ‘It was because Simon did not send him the money and the sons, that Jonathan perished.’

text.DRB: And he sent the children, and the hundred talents: and he lied, and dismissed not Jonathas.
text.KJV: So he sent them the children, and the hundred talents: Howbeit dissembled, neither would he let Ionathan goe.
text.NABRE: So he sent the boys and the hundred talents; but Trypho broke his promise and would not release Jonathan.
text.NRSV: So he sent the sons and the hundred talents, but Trypho broke his word and did not release Jonathan.

text.DRB: And after these things came Tryphon within the country, to destroy it, and they compassed by the way that leadeth to Addar, and Simon, & his camp marched into every place whither soever they went.
text.KJV: And after this came Tryphon to inuade the land, and destroy it, going round about by the way that leadeth vnto Adora, but Simon and his host marched against him in euery place wheresoeuer he went.
text.NABRE: Next Trypho moved to invade and ravage the country. His troops went around by the road that leads to Adora, but Simon and his army moved along opposite him everywhere he went.
text.NRSV: After this Trypho came to invade the country and destroy it, and he circled around by the way to Adora. But Simon and his army kept marching along opposite him to every place he went.

text.DRB: And they that were in the castle sent legates to Tryphon, that he should hasten to come through the desert, and should send them victuals.
text.KJV: Now they that were in the towre, sent messengers vnto Tryphon, to the end that he should hasten his comming vnto them by the wildernesse, and send them victuals.
text.NABRE: The people in the citadel kept sending emissaries to Trypho, pressing him to come to them by way of the wilderness, and to send them provisions.
text.NRSV: Now the men in the citadel kept sending envoys to Trypho urging him to come to them by way of the wilderness and to send them food.

text.DRB: And Tryphon prepared all the horsemen to come that night: but there was an exceeding great snow, and he came not into the country of Galaad.
text.KJV: Wherefore Tryphon made readie all his horsemen to come that night, but there fell a very great snow, by reason whereof he came not: So he departed & came into the countrey of Galaad.
text.NABRE: Although Trypho got all his cavalry ready to go, there was a very heavy snowfall that night, and he could not go on account of the snow. So he left for Gilead.
text.NRSV: So Trypho got all his cavalry ready to go, but that night a very heavy snow fell, and he did not go because of the snow. He marched off and went into the land of Gilead.

text.KJV: And when he came neere to Bascama, he slew Ionathan, who was buried there.
text.NABRE: When he was approaching Baskama, he had Jonathan killed and buried him there.
text.NRSV: When he approached Baskama, he killed Jonathan, and he was buried there.
text.DRB: And when he had approached to Bascama, he slew Jonathas and his sons there.
And Tryphon returned, and went into his country.
text.KJV: Afterward Tryphon returned, and went into his owne land.
text.NABRE: Then Trypho returned to his own land.
text.NRSV: Then Trypho turned and went back to his own land.

text.DRB: And Simon sent and took the bones of Jonathas his brother, and buried them in Modin the city of his fathers.
text.KJV: Then sent Simon and tooke the bones of Ionathan his brother, and buried them in Modin the citie of his fathers.
text.NABRE: Jonathan’s Tomb. Simon sent for the remains of his brother Jonathan, and buried him in Modein, the city of his ancestors.
text.NRSV: Jonathan’s Tomb Simon sent and took the bones of his brother Jonathan, and buried him in Modein, the city of his ancestors.

text.DRB: And all Israel lamented him with great lamentation: and they mourned for him many days.
text.KJV: And all Israel made great lamentation for him, and bewailed him many daies.
text.NABRE: All Israel bewailed him with solemn lamentation, mourning over him for many days.
text.NRSV: All Israel bewailed him with great lamentation, and mourned for him for many days.

text.DRB: And Simon built over the sepulchre of his father and of his brethren a building high to behold, of stone polished behind and before:
text.KJV: Simon also built a monument vpon the Sepulchre of his father and his brethren, and raised it aloft to the sight, with hewen stone behind and before.
text.NABRE: Then Simon erected over the tomb of his father and his brothers a monument of stones, polished front and back, and raised high enough to be seen at a distance.
text.NRSV: And Simon built a monument over the tomb of his father and his brothers; he made it high so that it might be seen, with polished stone at the front and back.

text.DRB: and he erected seven spires one against one, to his father and mother, and to his four brethren:
text.KJV: Moreouer hee set vp seuen pyramides one against another, for his father and his mother, and his foure brethren.
text.NABRE: He set up seven pyramids facing one another for his father and his mother and his four brothers.
text.NRSV: He also erected seven pyramids, opposite one another, for his father and mother and four brothers.

text.DRB: and about these he set great pillars: and upon the pillars armour for an eternal memory: and by the armour ships graven, which might be seen of all that sailed the sea.
text.KJV: And in these he made running deuices, about the which he set great pillars, and vpon the pillars he made all their armour for a perpetuall memory, and by the armour, ships carued, that they might be seene of all that saile on the sea.
text.NABRE: For the pyramids he devised a setting of massive columns, which he adorned with suits of armor as a perpetual memorial, and next to the armor carved ships, which could be seen by all who sailed the sea.
text.NRSV: For the pyramids he devised an elaborate setting, erecting about them great columns, and on the columns he put suits of armour for a permanent memorial, and beside the suits of armour he carved ships, so that they could be seen by all who sail the sea.

text.DRB: This is the sepulchre, that he made in Modin even until this day.
text.KJV: This is the Sepulchre which he made at Modin, and it standeth yet vnto this day.
text.NABRE: This tomb which he built at Modein is there to the present day.
text.NRSV: This is the tomb that he built in Modein; it remains to this day.

text.DRB: But Tryphon when he journeyed with Antiochus the king, being but a young man, by guile he slew him.
text.KJV: Now Tryphon dealt deceitfully with the yong king Antiochus, and slew him,
text.NABRE: Alliance of Simon and Demetrius II. Trypho dealt treacherously with the young King Antiochus. He killed him
text.NRSV: Judea Gains Independence Trypho dealt treacherously with the young King Antiochus; he killed him

text.DRB: And he reigned in his place, & put the crown of Asia upon himself, and made a great plague in the land.
text.KJV: And he raigned in his stead, and crowned himselfe king of Asia, and brought a great calamitie vpō the land.
text.NABRE: and became king in his place, putting on the crown of Asia. Thus he brought much evil on the land.
text.NRSV: and became king in his place, putting on the crown of Asia; and he brought great calamity on the land.

text.DRB: And Simon built the fortresses of Jurie, fortifying them with high towers, & great walls, and gates, and locks: and he put victuals in the fortresses.
text.KJV: Then Simon built vp the strong holds in Iudea, and fensed them about with high towres, and great walles and gates and barres, and layd vp victuals therein.
text.NABRE: Simon, for his part, built up the strongholds of Judea, fortifying them all around with high towers, thick walls, and gates with bars, and he stored up provisions in the strongholds.
text.NRSV: But Simon built up the strongholds of Judea and walled them all round, with high towers and great walls and gates and bolts, and he stored food in the strongholds.

text.DRB: And Simon chose men & sent to Demetrius the king, that he would make a release to the country: because all Tryphon's acts had been done by spoil.
text.KJV: Moreouer Simon chose men, and sent to king Demetrius, to the end he should giue the land an immunitie, because all that Tryphon did, was to spoyle.
text.NABRE: Simon also chose men and sent them to King Demetrius to obtain for the land an exemption from taxation, since Trypho did nothing but plunder.
text.NRSV: Simon also chose emissaries and sent them to King Demetrius with a request to grant relief to the country, for all that Trypho did was to plunder.

text.DRB: And Demetrius the king according to these words answered him, and wrote an epistle in this manner:
text.KJV: Unto whom king Demetrius answered and wrote after this maner.
text.NABRE: King Demetrius replied favorably and sent him the following letter:
text.NRSV: King Demetrius sent him a favourable reply to this request, and wrote him a letter as follows,

text.DRB: King Demetrius to Simon the High-Priest, and the friend of kings, and to the ancients, and to the nation of the Jews, greeting.
text.KJV: King Demetrius vnto Simon the high Priest, and friend of kings, as also vnto the Elders and nation of the Iewes, sendeth greeting.
text.NABRE: “King Demetrius sends greetings to Simon, high priest and friend of kings, and to the elders and the Jewish people.
text.NRSV: ‘King Demetrius to Simon, the high priest and friend of kings, and to the elders and nation of the Jews, greetings.

text.DRB: The crown of gold, and the bahem, which you sent, we have received: and we are ready to make with you great peace, and to write to the king's chief officers to release you the things that we have released.
text.KJV: The golden crowne, and the scarlet robe which ye sent vnto vs, we haue receiued, and wee are ready to make a stedfast peace with you, yea and to write vnto our officers to confirme the immunities which we haue granted.
text.NABRE: We have received the gold crown and the palm branch that you sent. We are ready to make a lasting peace with you and to write to our officials to grant you exemption.
text.NRSV: We have received the gold crown and the palm branch that you sent, and we are ready to make a general peace with you and to write to our officials to grant you release from tribute.

text.DRB: For whatsoever things we have decreed unto you, stand in force. The holds that you have built, let them be to you.
text.KJV: And whatsoeuer couenants we haue made with you, shall stand, and the strong holdes which yee haue builded shalbe your owne.
text.NABRE: Whatever decrees we have made in your regard remain in force, and the strongholds that you have built you may keep.
text.NRSV: All the grants that we have made to you remain valid, and let the strongholds that you have built be your possession.

text.DRB: We remit also the ignorances, and offences even until this day, and the crown which you owed: and if any other thing was tributary in Jerusalem, now let it not be tributary.
text.KJV: As for any ouersight or fault committed vnto this day, we forgiue it, and the crowne taxe also which yee owe vs, if there were any other tribute paide in Ierusalem, it shall no more be paide.
text.NABRE: We pardon any oversights and offenses committed up to now, as well as the crown tax that you owe. Any other tax that used to be collected in Jerusalem shall no longer be collected there.
text.NRSV: We pardon any errors and offences committed to this day, and cancel the crown tax that you owe; and whatever other tax has been collected in Jerusalem shall be collected no longer.

text.DRB: And if any of you be fit to be enrolled among ours, let them be enrolled and let there be peace between us.
text.KJV: And looke who are meet among you to be in our court, let them be inrolled, and let there be peace betwixt vs.
text.NABRE: Any of you qualified for enrollment in our service may be enrolled. Let there be peace between us.”
text.NRSV: And if any of you are qualified to be enrolled in our bodyguard, let them be enrolled, and let there be peace between us.’

text.KJV: Thus the yoke of the heathen was taken away from Israel, in the hundred and seuentieth yeere.
text.NABRE: Thus in the one hundred and seventieth year, the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,
text.NRSV: In the one hundred and seventieth year the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,
text.DRB: In the year an hundreth seventy was the yoke of the Gentiles taken away from Israel.

text.KJV: Then the people of Israel began to write in their instruments, and contracts, in the first yeere of Simon the high Priest, the gouernour, and leader of the Iewes.
text.NABRE: and the people began to write in their records and contracts, “In the first year of Simon, great high priest, governor, and leader of the Jews.”
text.NRSV: and the people began to write in their documents and contracts, ‘In the first year of Simon the great high priest and commander and leader of the Jews.’
text.DRB: And the people of Israel began to write in the tables, and public instruments. The first year under Simon the High-Priest, the great Duke, and the Prince of the Jews:
In these days Simon approached to Gaza, and compassed it with camps, and he made engines, and set them to the city, and he struck one tower, and took it.
text.KJV: In those dayes Simon camped against Gaza, and besieged it round about; he made also an engine of warre, and set it by the city, and battered a certaine towre, and tooke it.
text.NABRE: Simon Captures Gazara. In those days Simon besieged Gazara and surrounded it with troops. He made a siege machine, brought it up against the city, and attacked and captured one of the towers.
text.NRSV: The Capture of Gazara by Simon In those days Simon encamped against Gazara and surrounded it with troops. He made a siege-engine, brought it up to the city, and battered and captured one tower.

text.DRB: And they that were within the engine, brake out into the city: and there was made a great stir in the city.
text.KJV: And they that were in the Engine leapt into the citie, whereupon there was a great vproare in the citie:
text.NABRE: Those in the siege machine leaped down into the city and a great tumult arose there.
text.NRSV: The men in the siege-engine leapt out into the city, and a great tumult arose in the city.

text.DRB: And they that were in the city went up with their wives and children upon the wall their coats rent, and they cried with a loud voice, desiring of Simon that the right hands might be given them,
text.KJV: Insomuch as the people of the citie rent their clothes, and climed vpon the walles, with their wiues and children, and cried with a lowd voice, beseeching Simon to grant them peace.
text.NABRE: Those in the city, together with their wives and children, went up on the wall, with their garments rent, and cried out in loud voices, begging Simon to grant them terms of peace.
text.NRSV: The men in the city, with their wives and children, went up on the wall with their clothes torn, and they cried out with a loud voice, asking Simon to make peace with them;

text.DRB: and they said: Requite us not according to our malices, but according to thy mercies.
text.KJV: And they said, Deale not with vs according to our wickednesse, but according to thy mercy.
text.NABRE: They said, “Treat us not according to our evil deeds but according to your mercy.”
text.NRSV: they said, ‘Do not treat us according to our wicked acts but according to your mercy.’

text.DRB: And Simon yielding destroyed them not: but yet he cast them out of the city, and cleansed the houses, wherein there had been idols, and then he entered into it with hymns blessing our Lord:
text.KJV: So Simon was appeased towards them, and fought no more against them, but put them out of the citie, and cleansed the houses wherein the idols were: and so entred into it, with songs, and thankesgiuing.
text.NABRE: So Simon came to terms with them and did not attack them. He expelled them from the city, however, and he purified the houses in which there were idols. Then he entered the city with hymns and songs of praise.
text.NRSV: So Simon reached an agreement with them and stopped fighting against them. But he expelled them from the city and cleansed the houses in which the idols were located, and then entered it with hymns and praise.

text.DRB: and all filthiness being cast out of it, he placed in it men that should do the law: and he fortified it, and made it his habitation.
text.KJV: Yea, he put all vncleannesse out of it, and placed such men there, as would keepe the Law, and made it stronger then it was before, and built therein a dwelling place for himselfe.
text.NABRE: After removing from it everything that was impure, he settled there people who observed the law. He improved its fortifications and built himself a residence.
text.NRSV: He removed all uncleanness from it, and settled in it those who observed the law. He also strengthened its fortifications and built in it a house for himself.

text.DRB: But they that were in the castle of Jerusalem, were prohibited to go out and to come into the country, and to buy and sell: and they were exceeding hungry, and many of them perished for famine,
text.KJV: They also of the towre in Ierusalem were kept so strait, that they could neither come foorth, nor goe into the countrey, nor buy, nor sell, wherefore they were in great distresse for want of victuals, and a great number of them perished through famine.
text.NABRE: Simon Captures the Citadel. The people in the citadel in Jerusalem were prevented from going out into the country and back to buy or sell; they suffered greatly from hunger, and many of them died of starvation.
text.NRSV: Simon Regains the Citadel at Jerusalem Those who were in the citadel at Jerusalem were prevented from going in and out to buy and sell in the country. So they were very hungry, and many of them perished from famine.

text.DRB: and they cried to Simon to receive the right hands, and he gave it them: and he cast them out from thence, and cleansed the castle from the contaminations:
text.KJV: Then cried they to Simon, beseeching him to bee at one with them, which thing hee graunted them, and when he had put them out from thence, he cleansed the towre from pollutions:
text.NABRE: They finally cried out to Simon, and he gave them terms of peace. He expelled them from the citadel and cleansed it of impurities.
text.NRSV: Then they cried to Simon to make peace with them, and he did so. But he expelled them from there and cleansed the citadel from its pollutions.

text.DRB: and they entered into it the three and twentieth day of the second month, the year an hundreth seventy one, with praise, and boughs of palm trees, and harps, and cymbals, and nables, and hymns, and canticles, because the great enemy was destroyed out of Israel.
text.KJV: And entred into it the three and twentieth day of the second moneth, in the hundred seuentie and one yere, with thankesgiuing, and branches of palme trees, and with harpes, and cymbals, and with viols and hymnes, and songs: because there was destroyed a great enemy out of Israel.
text.NABRE: On the twenty-third day of the second month, in the one hundred and seventy-first year, the Jews entered the citadel with shouts of praise, the waving of palm branches, the playing of harps and cymbals and lyres, and the singing of hymns and canticles, because a great enemy of Israel had been crushed.
text.NRSV: On the twenty-third day of the second month, in the one hundred and seventy-first year, the Jews entered it with praise and palm branches, and with harps and cymbals and stringed instruments, and with hymns and songs, because a great enemy had been crushed and removed from Israel.

text.DRB: And he ordained that every year these days should be kept with gladness.
text.KJV: Hee ordained also that that day should be kept euery yeere with gladnes. Moreouer, the hill of the Temple that was by the towre he made stronger then it was, and there hee dwelt himselfe with his company.
text.NABRE: Simon decreed that this day should be celebrated every year with rejoicing. He also strengthened the fortifications of the temple mount alongside the citadel, and he and his people dwelt there.
text.NRSV: Simon decreed that every year they should celebrate this day with rejoicing. He strengthened the fortifications of the temple hill alongside the citadel, and he and his men lived there.

text.DRB: And he fortified the mount of the temple, that was by the castle, and he dwelt there himself, & they that were with him.
text.KJV: And when Simon sawe that Iohn his sonne was a valiant man, he made him captaine of all the hostes and dwelt in Gazara.
text.NABRE: Seeing that his son John was now a grown man, Simon made him commander of all his soldiers, and he dwelt in Gazara.
text.NRSV: Simon saw that his son John had reached manhood, and so he made him commander of all the forces; and he lived at Gazara.

text.DRB: And Simon saw John his son, that he was a valiant man of war: and he made him captain of all the hosts: and he dwelt in Gazara.
In the year an hundreth seventy two, king Demetrius gathered his army, and went into Media to get him aids to expugn Tryphon.
text.KJV: Now in the hundred threescore and twelfth yeere, king Demetrius gathered his forces together, and went into Media, to get him helpe to fight against Tryphon.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Capture of Demetrius II. In the one hundred and seventy-second year, King Demetrius assembled his army and marched into Media to obtain help so that he could fight Trypho.
text.NRSV: Capture of Demetrius 14

text.DRB: And Arsaces the king of Persia, and Media heard, that Demetrius was entered his confines, and he sent one of his princes to take him alive.
text.KJV: But when Arsaces the king of Persia & Media, heard that Demetrius was entred within his borders, he sent one of his princes to take him aliue.
text.NABRE: When Arsaces, king of Persia and Media, heard that Demetrius had entered his territory, he sent one of his generals to take him alive.
text.NRSV: When King Arsaces of Persia and Media heard that Demetrius had invaded his territory, he sent one of his generals to take him alive.

text.DRB: And he went, and struck the camp of Demetrius: and took him, and brought him to Arsaces, and he put him into ward.
text.KJV: Who went and smote the hoste of Demetrius, and tooke him and brought him to Arsaces, by whom hee was put in warde.
text.NABRE: The general went forth and attacked the army of Demetrius; he captured him and brought him to Arsaces, who put him under guard.
text.NRSV: The general went and defeated the army of Demetrius, and seized him and took him to Arsaces, who put him under guard.

text.KJV: As for the land of Iudea, that was quiet all the dayes of Simon: for he sought the good of his nation, in such wise, as that euermore his authoritie and honour pleased them well.
text.NABRE: Praise of Simon The land was at rest all the days of Simon, who sought the good of his nation. His rule delighted his people and his glory all his days.
text.NRSV: Eulogy of Simon The land had rest all the days of Simon. He sought the good of his nation; his rule was pleasing to them, as was the honour shown him, all his days.
text.DRB: And all the land of Juda was at rest all the days of Simon, and he sought good to his nation: & his power, and his glory pleased them all days.
And with all his glory he took Joppe for an haven, and made it an entrance unto the isles of the sea.
text.KJV: And as he was honourable (in all his acts) so in this, that he tooke Ioppe for an hauen, and made an entrance to the yles of the Sea,
text.NABRE: As his crowning glory he took Joppa for a port and made it a gateway to the isles of the sea.
text.NRSV: To crown all his honours he took Joppa for a harbour, and opened a way to the isles of the sea.

text.DRB: And he enlarged the borders of his nation, and obtained the country:
text.KJV: And enlarged the boundes of his nation, and recouered the countrey,
text.NABRE: He enlarged the borders of his nation and gained control of the country.
text.NRSV: He extended the borders of his nation, and gained full control of the country.

text.DRB: And gathered a great captivity, and had dominion of Gazara, and of Bethsura, and of the castle: and took away the uncleanness out of it, and there was none that resisted him.
text.KJV: And gathered together a great number of captiues, and had the dominion of Gazara and Bethsura, and the towre, out of the which he tooke all vncleannesse, neither was there any that resisted him.
text.NABRE: He took many prisoners of war and made himself master of Gazara, Beth-zur, and the citadel. He cleansed the citadel of its impurities; there was no one to withstand him.
text.NRSV: He gathered a host of captives; he ruled over Gazara and Beth-zur and the citadel, and he removed its uncleanness from it; and there was none to oppose him.

text.DRB: And every man tilled his land with peace: and the land of Juda yielded her fruits, and the trees of the fields their fruit.
text.KJV: Then did they till their ground in peace, and the earth gaue her increase, and the trees of the field their fruit.
text.NABRE: The people cultivated their land in peace; the land yielded its produce, the trees of the field their fruit.
text.NRSV: They tilled their land in peace; the ground gave its increase, and the trees of the plains their fruit.

text.DRB: The ancients sat all in the streets, and treated of the good things of the land, and the youngmen did on them glory, and the stoles of war.
text.KJV: The ancient men sate all in the streetes, communing together of good things, and the young men put on glorious and warrelike apparell.
text.NABRE: Old men sat in the squares, all talking about the good times, while the young men put on the glorious raiment of war.
text.NRSV: Old men sat in the streets; they all talked together of good things, and the youths put on splendid military attire.

text.DRB: And he gave victuals to the cities, and he appointed them that they should be vessels of munition, till the name of his glory was renowned even to the end of the earth.
text.KJV: He prouided victuals for the cities, and set in them all maner of munition, so that his honourable name was renowmed vnto the end of the world.
text.NABRE: He supplied the cities with food and equipped them with means of defense, till his glorious name reached the ends of the earth.
text.NRSV: He supplied the towns with food, and furnished them with the means of defence, until his renown spread to the ends of the earth.

text.DRB: He made peace upon the land, and Israel rejoiced with great joy.
text.KJV: He made peace in the land, and Israel reioyced with great ioy:
text.NABRE: He brought peace to the land, and Israel was filled with great joy.
text.NRSV: He established peace in the land, and Israel rejoiced with great joy.

text.DRB: And every man sat under his vine, and under his figtree: and there was none to make them afraid.
text.KJV: For euery man sate vnder his vine, and his figgetree, and there was none to fray them:
text.NABRE: Every one sat under his vine and fig tree, with no one to disturb them.
text.NRSV: All the people sat under their own vines and fig trees, and there was none to make them afraid.

text.DRB: There was none left that impugned them upon the earth: kings were discomfited in those days.
text.KJV: Neither was there any left in the lande to fight against them: yea, the Kings themselues were ouerthrowen in those dayes.
text.NABRE: No attacker was left in the land; the kings in those days were crushed.
text.NRSV: No one was left in the land to fight them, and the kings were crushed in those days.

text.DRB: And he confirmed all the humble of his people, and sought the law, & took away every wicked and evil man:
text.KJV: Moreouer hee strengthened all those of his people that were brought low: the Law he searched out, and euery contemner of the Law, and wicked person, he tooke away.
text.NABRE: He strengthened all the lowly among his people and was zealous for the law; he destroyed the lawless and the wicked.
text.NRSV: He gave help to all the humble among his people; he sought out the law, and did away with all the renegades and outlaws.

text.DRB: he glorified the holies, and multiplied the vessels of the holy places.
text.KJV: He beautified the Sanctuary, and multiplied the vessels of the Temple.
text.NABRE: The sanctuary he made splendid and multiplied its furnishings.
text.NRSV: He made the sanctuary glorious, and added to the vessels of the sanctuary.

text.DRB: And it was heard at Rome that Jonathas was dead, & even unto the Spartiates: and they were very sorry.
text.KJV: Now when it was heard at Rome, & as far as Sparta, that Ionathan was dead, they were very sorie.
text.NABRE: Alliance with Rome and Sparta. When people in Rome and even in Sparta heard that Jonathan had died, they were deeply grieved.
text.NRSV: Diplomacy with Rome and Sparta It was heard in Rome, and as far away as Sparta, that Jonathan had died, and they were deeply grieved.

text.DRB: But as they heard that Simon his brother was made the High-Priest in his place, and he obtained all the country, and the cities therein;
text.KJV: But assoone as they heard that his brother Simon was made high Priest in his stead, and ruled the countrey, and the cities therein,
text.NABRE: But when they heard that his brother Simon had become high priest in his place and was master of the territory and its cities,
text.NRSV: When they heard that his brother Simon had become high priest in his stead, and that he was ruling over the country and the towns in it,

text.DRB: they wrote to him in brazen tables, to renew the amities and society, which they had made with Judas, & with Jonathas his brethren.
text.KJV: They wrote vnto him in tables of brasse, to renew the friendship & league which they had made with Iudas and Ionathan his brethren:
text.NABRE: they sent him inscribed tablets of bronze to renew with him the friendship and alliance that they had established with his brothers Judas and Jonathan.
text.NRSV: they wrote to him on bronze tablets to renew with him the friendship and alliance that they had established with his brothers Judas and Jonathan.

text.DRB: And they were read in the sight of the Church in Jerusalem. And this is a copy of the epistles, that the Spartiates sent.
text.KJV: Which writings were read before the Congregation at Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: These were read before the assembly in Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: And these were read before the assembly in Jerusalem.

text.DRB: The Princes and the cities of the Spartians to Simon the grand Priest, & to the ancients, and the Priests, and the rest of the people of the Jews their brethren, greeting.
text.KJV: And this is the copy of the letters that the Lacedemonians sent: The rulers of the Lacedemonians, with the city, vnto Simon the high Priest, and the Elders and Priestes, and residue of the people of the Iewes, our brethren, send greeting.
text.NABRE: This is a copy of the letter that the Spartans sent: “The rulers and the city of the Spartans send greetings to Simon the high priest, the elders, the priests, and the rest of the Jewish people, our brothers.
text.NRSV: This is a copy of the letter that the Spartans sent: ‘The rulers and the city of the Spartans to the high priest Simon and to the elders and the priests and the rest of the Jewish people, our brothers, greetings.

text.DRB: The legates, that were sent to our people, have told us of your glory, and honour, and joy: and we rejoiced at their entrance.
text.KJV: The Embassadors that were sent vnto our people, certified vs of your glory and honour, wherefore we were glad of their comming,
text.NABRE: The ambassadors sent to our people have informed us of your glory and renown, and we rejoiced at their coming.
text.NRSV: The envoys who were sent to our people have told us about your glory and honour, and we rejoiced at their coming.

text.DRB: And we wrote that, which was said of them in the counsels of the people, thus: Numenius the son of Antiochus, and Antipater the son of Jason, legates of the Jews, came to us, renewing with us old amity.
text.KJV: And did register the things that they spake, in the counsell of the people, in this maner: Numenius sonne of Antiochus, and Antipater sonne of Iason, the Iewes Embassadours, came vnto vs, to renew the friendship they had with vs.
text.NABRE: In accordance with what they said we have recorded the following in the public decrees: Numenius, son of Antiochus, and Antipater, son of Jason, ambassadors of the Jews, have come to us to renew their friendship with us.
text.NRSV: We have recorded what they said in our public decrees, as follows, “Numenius son of Antiochus and Antipater son of Jason, envoys of the Jews, have come to us to renew their friendship with us.

text.DRB: And it pleased the people to receive the men gloriously, and to put a copy of their words in the severed book of the people, to be for a memory to the people of the Spartiates. And a copy of these we have written to Simon the grand Priest.
text.KJV: And it pleased the people to entertaine the men honourably, and to put the copy of their embassage in publike records, to the end the people of the Lacedemonians might haue a memoriall therof: furthermore we haue written a copy thereof vnto Simon the hie Priest.
text.NABRE: The people have resolved to receive these men with honor, and to deposit a copy of their words in the public archives, so that the people of Sparta may have a record of them. A copy of this decree has been made for Simon the high priest.”
text.NRSV: It has pleased our people to receive these men with honour and to put a copy of their words in the public archives, so that the people of the Spartans may have a record of them. And they have sent a copy of this to the high priest Simon.”’

text.DRB: And after these things Simon sent Numenius to Rome, having a great buckler of gold, of the weight of thousand pounds, to establish amity with them. But when the people of Rome had heard
text.KJV: After this, Simon sent Numenius to Rome, with a great shield of golde of a thousand pound weight, to confirme the league with them.
text.NABRE: After this, Simon sent Numenius to Rome with a large gold shield weighing a thousand minas, to confirm the alliance with the Romans.
text.NRSV: After this Simon sent Numenius to Rome with a large gold shield weighing one thousand minas, to confirm the alliance with the Romans.

text.DRB: these words, they said: What thanks-giving shall we render to Simon & his children?
text.KJV: Whereof when the people heard, they said, What thankes shall wee giue to Simon and his sonnes?
text.NABRE: Official Honors for Simon. When the people heard of these things, they said, “How shall we thank Simon and his sons?
text.NRSV: Official Honours for Simon When the people heard these things they said, ‘How shall we thank Simon and his sons?

text.DRB: For he hath restored his brethren, and hath expugned the enemies of Israel from them: and they decreed him liberty, & registered it in tables of brass, & put it in titles in mount Sion.
text.KJV: For hee and his brethren, and the house of his father, haue established Israel, and chased away in fight their enemies from them, and confirmed their libertie.
text.NABRE: He and his brothers and his father’s house have stood firm and repulsed Israel’s enemies, and so have established its freedom.” So they made an inscription on bronze tablets, which they affixed to pillars on Mount Zion.
text.NRSV: For he and his brothers and the house of his father have stood firm; they have fought and repulsed Israel’s enemies and established its freedom.’

text.KJV: So then they wrote in tables of brasse, which they set vpon pillars in mount Sion, and this is the copie of the writing. The eighteenth day of the moneth Elul, in the hundred threescore and twelft yeere, being the third yeere of Simon the hie priest,
text.NABRE: The following is a copy of the inscription: “On the eighteenth day of Elul, in the one hundred and seventy-second year, that is, the third year under Simon the great high priest in Asaramel,
text.NRSV: So they made a record on bronze tablets and put it on pillars on Mount Zion. This is a copy of what they wrote: ‘On the eighteenth day of Elul, in the one hundred and seventy-second year, which is the third year of the great high priest Simon,
text.DRB: And this is a copy of the writing. The Eighteenth day of the month Elul, in the year an hundreth seventy two, the third year under Simon the grand-Priest in Asaramel,
in a great assembly of the Priests, and of the people & the Princes of the nation, and the ancients of the country, these things were notified: that there have often been battles made in our country.
text.KJV: At Saramel in the great congregation of the priests and people, and rulers of the nation, & elders of the country, were these things notified vnto vs.
text.NABRE: in a great assembly of priests, people, rulers of the nation, and elders of the region, the following proclamation was made to us:
text.NRSV: in Asaramel, in the great assembly of the priests and the people and the rulers of the nation and the elders of the country, the following was proclaimed to us:

text.DRB: But Simon the son of Mathathias of the children of Jarib, & his brethren put themselves in danger, and resisted the adversaries of their nation, that their holies and law might stand: and with great glory have they glorified their nation.
text.KJV: Forsomuch as often times there haue bin warres in the countrey, wherin for the maintenance of their Sanctuarie, and the law, Simon the sonne of Mattathias of the posteritie of Iarib, together with his brethren, put themselues in ieopardie, and resisting the enemies of their nation, did their nation great honour.
text.NABRE: “‘Since there have often been wars in our country, Simon, son of the priest Mattathias, descendant of Joarib, and his brothers have put themselves in danger and resisted the enemies of their nation, so that their sanctuary and law might be maintained, and they have thus brought great glory to their nation.
text.NRSV: ‘Since wars often occurred in the country, Simon son of Mattathias, a priest of the sons of Joarib, and his brothers, exposed themselves to danger and resisted the enemies of their nation, in order that their sanctuary and the law might be preserved; and they brought great glory to their nation.

text.DRB: And Jonathas gathered together his nation, & was made their grand Priest, & he was laid to his people.
text.KJV: (For after that Ionathan hauing gathered his nation together, and bene their hie priest, was added to his people,
text.NABRE: Jonathan rallied the nation, became their high priest, and was gathered to his people.
text.NRSV: Jonathan rallied the nation, became their high priest, and was gathered to his people.

text.DRB: And their enemies would conculcate, and destroy their country, and extend their hands against the holies.
text.KJV: Their enemies purposed to inuade their countrey that they might destroy it, and lay hands on the Sanctuary.
text.NABRE: When their enemies sought to invade and ravage their country and to violate their sanctuary,
text.NRSV: When their enemies decided to invade their country and lay hands on their sanctuary,

text.DRB: Then Simon, resisted and fought for his nation, and bestowed much money, and armed the valiant men of his nation, and gave them wages:
text.KJV: At which time Simon rose vp, and fought for his nation, and spent much of his own substance, & armed the valiant men of his nation, & gaue them wages,
text.NABRE: Simon rose up and fought for his nation, spending large sums of his own money to equip his nation’s forces and give them their pay.
text.NRSV: then Simon rose up and fought for his nation. He spent great sums of his own money; he armed the soldiers of his nation and paid them wages.

text.DRB: and he fortified the cities of Jurie, and Bethsura, that was in the borders of Jurie, where the armour of the enemies was before: and he placed there Jews for a garrison.
text.KJV: And fortified the cities of Iudea, together with Bethsura that lieth vpon the borders of Iudea, where the armour of the enemies had bin before, but he set a garison of Iewes there.
text.NABRE: He fortified the cities of Judea, especially the border city of Beth-zur, formerly the site of the enemy’s weaponry, and he stationed there a garrison of Jewish soldiers.
text.NRSV: He fortified the towns of Judea, and Beth-zur on the borders of Judea, where formerly the arms of the enemy had been stored, and he placed there a garrison of Jews.

text.DRB: And he fortified Joppe, which was by the sea: and Gazara, which is in the borders of Azotus, wherein the enemies dwelt before, and he placed Jews there: and whatsoever was fit for their correption, he put in them.
text.KJV: Moreouer, hee fortified Ioppe which lieth vpon the Sea, and Gazara that bordereth vpon Azotus, where the enemies had dwelt before: but hee placed Iewes there, and furnished them with all things conuenient for the reparation thereof.)
text.NABRE: He also fortified Joppa by the sea and Gazara on the border of Azotus, a place previously occupied by the enemy; these cities he settled with Jews and furnished them with all that was necessary for their restoration.
text.NRSV: He also fortified Joppa, which is by the sea, and Gazara, which is on the borders of Azotus, where the enemy formerly lived. He settled Jews there, and provided in those towns whatever was necessary for their restoration.

text.DRB: And the people saw the fact of Simon, and the glory that he meant to do to his nation, and they made him their duke, and grand Priest, for that he had done all these things, and for the justice, and faith, which he kept to his nation, and for that he sought by all means to advance his people.
text.KJV: The people therefore seeing the acts of Simon, and vnto what glory he thought to bring his nation, made him their gouernor and chiefe priest, because he had done all these things, and for the iustice and faith which hee kept to his nation, and for that hee sought by all meanes to exalt his people.
text.NABRE: When the people saw Simon’s fidelity and the glory he planned to bring to his nation, they made him their leader and high priest because of all he had accomplished and the justice and fidelity he had shown his nation. In every way he sought to exalt his people.
text.NRSV: ‘The people saw Simon’s faithfulness and the glory that he had resolved to win for his nation, and they made him their leader and high priest, because he had done all these things and because of the justice and loyalty that he had maintained towards his nation. He sought in every way to exalt his people.

text.DRB: And in his days it prospered in his hands, so that the heathen were taken away out of their country, and they that were in the city of David in Jerusalem in the castle, out of which they came forth, and contaminated all things that were round about the holies, and they brought great plague upon chastity:
text.KJV: For in his time things prospered in his hands, so that the heathen were taken out of their countrey, and they also that were in the citie of Dauid in Ierusalem, who had made themselues a towre, out of which they issued, and polluted all about the Sanctuarie, and did much hurt in the holy place.
text.NABRE: “‘In his time and under his guidance they succeeded in driving the Gentiles out of their country and those in the City of David in Jerusalem, who had built for themselves a citadel from which they used to sally forth to defile the environs of the sanctuary and inflict grave injury on its purity.
text.NRSV: In his days things prospered in his hands, so that the Gentiles were put out of the country, as were also those in the city of David in Jerusalem, who had built themselves a citadel from which they used to sally forth and defile the environs of the sanctuary, doing great damage to its purity.

text.DRB: and he placed in it men of Jurie to the defence of the country, and of the city, and he raised the walls of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: But he placed Iewes therein, and fortified it for the safetie of the countrey, and the city, and raised vp the wals of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: In this citadel he stationed Jewish soldiers, and he strengthened its fortifications for the security of the land and the city, while he also built up the wall of Jerusalem to a greater height.
text.NRSV: He settled Jews in it and fortified it for the safety of the country and of the city, and built the walls of Jerusalem higher.

text.DRB: And king Demetrius appointed him the high priesthood.
text.KJV: King Demetrius also confirmed him in the high priesthood, according to those things,
text.NABRE: Consequently, King Demetrius confirmed him in the high priesthood,
text.NRSV: ‘In view of these things King Demetrius confirmed him in the high-priesthood,

text.DRB: According to these things he made him his friend, and glorified him with great glory.
text.KJV: And made him one of his friends, and honoured him with great honour.
text.NABRE: made him one of his Friends, and conferred great honor on him.
text.NRSV: made him one of his Friends, and paid him high honours.

text.DRB: For he heard that the Jews were called of the Romans, friends, & fellows, & brethren, & that they received Simon's legates gloriously:
text.KJV: For he had heard say, that the Romanes had called the Iewes their friends, and confederates, and brethren, and that they had entertained the Embassadours of Simon honourably.
text.NABRE: This was because he had heard that the Romans had addressed the Jews as friends, allies, and brothers, that they had received Simon’s envoys with honor,
text.NRSV: For he had heard that the Jews were addressed by the Romans as friends and allies and brothers, and that the Romans had received the envoys of Simon with honour.

text.KJV: Also that the Iewes & priests were wel pleased that Simon should be their gouernour, and high priest for euer vntil there should arise a faithfull prophet.
text.NABRE: and that the Jewish people and their priests had decided the following: Simon shall be their leader and high priest forever until a trustworthy prophet arises.
text.NRSV: ‘The Jews and their priests have resolved that Simon should be their leader and high priest for ever, until a trustworthy prophet should arise,
text.DRB: and that the Jews, and their Priests consented that he should be their duke, & high-Priest for ever, till there rise the faithful Prophet:
and that he be duke over them, and that he should have the care of the holies, and that he should appoint rulers over their works, and over the country, and over the armour, and over the holds.
text.KJV: Moreouer, that he should be their captaine, and should take charge of the Sanctuarie, to set them ouer their workes, and ouer the countrey, and ouer the armour, and ouer the fortresses, that (I say) he should take charge of the Sanctuarie.
text.NABRE: He shall act as governor over them, and shall have charge of the sanctuary, to make regulations concerning its functions and concerning the country, its weapons and strongholds.
text.NRSV: and that he should be governor over them and that he should take charge of the sanctuary and appoint officials over its tasks and over the country and the weapons and the strongholds, and that he should take charge of the sanctuary,

text.DRB: And that he have care of the holies: and that he be heard of all, and that all writings in the country be written in his name: and that he be clothed with purple and gold:
text.KJV: Besides this, that he should be obeyed of euery man, and that all the writings in the countrey should be made in his name, and that he should be clothed in purple, and weare gold.
